reviews.review_msg: This course was a complete waste of money. Main text was 125 pages, and concepts were extremely basic. Lectures were short and unclear, and added no value. Much of class time was used to work on assignments, but attendance and participation are necessary. She seems out of touch and her teaching style is not effective for third year students.

The comments are far to harsh. Surpringly the professor was kind and flexible with her time. Grades were not given out for free but the grading was fair. I did find her isntructions confusing from time to time but once again its easy to just ask her for clarification sinces shes easy to reach through email. Highly recomend this prof.

She isn't as bad as these comments say. She's not generous with grades because (hard to believe) you have to earn your score...  She has thoughtful comments on assignments about where to improve but by the high standard of published work. Admittedly she has unclear instructions at times, but she's nice and receptive to questions so just ask her...

Great lecturer, and he finds a way to make everybody feel good about their contributions in class - class is discussion heavy. You MUST do the readings, but otherwise the class is lighter-workload, no exams. He's also chill, funny, and a fair grader. You will get good marks if you thoughtfully engage with the material and follow the criteria.
a king tbh, a very open minded guy and he's funny in a dad way!!! very good at explaining and sweet

Sean is an incredible professor. He understands not everyone is comfortable speaking in front of the class, so for participation he accepts emailing your thoughts to him after class for participation marks. His lectures and material are engaging. Extremely understanding that you have a life outside of school and does his best to work around it.
